title: Cross-chain Merkle root sync via EIP-1186 storage proofs

artifacts.description: Core registry holding humanTree and agentTree Merkle roots; deploy on Base Sepolia, Arbitrum One, and Polygon PoS
Accepts EIP-1186 storage proofs and L1 block hash anchors to sync humanTree/agentTree roots from source chain without a trusted relayer
Thin adapter interface over each L2's native L1 state oracle (ArbSys on Arbitrum, StateCommitmentChain/StateSender on Polygon) to retrieve finalized L1 block hashes
Pure library implementing EIP-1186 Merkle-Patricia trie proof verification (account proof + storage proof) against a given stateRoot
Shared interface for L1 block hash oracle adapters across supported L2s
Hardhat tests: unit tests with forked Base Sepolia state, proof fixture generation via eth_getProof, and end-to-end relay simulation
Unit tests for MPT proof verification library against known-good EIP-1186 proof vectors
Captured eth_getProof JSON fixtures for humanTreeRoot and agentTreeRoot slots across Base Sepolia, Arbitrum, Polygon
TypeScript helpers: fetchStorageProof(rpcUrl, registryAddr, blockTag), encodeRelayCalldata(proof), and pollRootSync(targetChain) utility
Protocol spec: slot layout, proof encoding format, security assumptions (L1 finality window, reorg depth), and oracle trust model per L2
Operator guide: deploying IdentityRegistry + RootRelay on each chain, configuring oracle addresses, and running the relay flow

steps: Pin storage slot indices for humanTreeRoot and agentTreeRoot in IdentityRegistry using a fixed layout (avoid mappings at top-level); document slot numbers in spec
Implement StorageProofVerifier.sol: decode RLP-encoded EIP-1186 account proof and storage proof, verify against provided stateRoot using MPT trie node hashing
Implement IL1BlockHashOracle interface; write ArbSysAdapter (calls ArbSys.arbBlockHash for L1 block numbers) and PolygonStateAdapter (reads from Polygon's L1 state commitment contract)

Implement RootRelay.sol: accept (l1BlockNumber, accountProof, storageProof[humanRoot], storageProof[agentRoot]), retrieve stateRoot via L1BlockHashOracle, delegate verification to StorageProofVerifier, then write verified roots to local IdentityRegistry
Add staleness guard in RootRelay: reject relays where l1BlockNumber is older than MAX_ROOT_AGE blocks to prevent replaying stale roots
Deploy IdentityRegistry to Base Sepolia (source), Arbitrum Sepolia, and Polygon Amoy testnets; record addresses in contracts/deployments/
Deploy RootRelay + oracle adapters on Arbitrum Sepolia and Polygon Amoy pointing at Base Sepolia as source
Capture eth_getProof fixtures: call eth_getProof on Base Sepolia IdentityRegistry for both root slots at a finalized block; store as JSON test fixtures
Write StorageProofVerifier unit tests against captured fixtures, including malformed-proof and wrong-root negative cases

Write RootRelay integration tests using Hardhat's network forking: fork Base Sepolia state, simulate ArbSys returning the forked block hash, call relayRoot with real proof, assert local registry updated
Implement SDK fetchStorageProof() using ethers/viem eth_getProof RPC call and encodeRelayCalldata() to ABI-encode the proof structs for RootRelay
Write cross-chain spec: document slot layout, finality assumptions per L2 (Arbitrum ~12 L1 blocks, Polygon checkpoint ~256 blocks), proof encoding, and oracle upgrade path
Add Hardhat deploy scripts for all three chains with network-specific oracle adapter selection
Write operator docs covering full relay flow: fetch proof from source RPC, call relayRoot on destination, verify roots match
dependencies: EIP-1186 eth_getProof RPC support on Base Sepolia, Arbitrum Sepolia, and Polygon Amoy nodes
Polygon L1 state commitment contract address on Amoy testnet
Hardhat network forking with archive node RPC for integration tests
RLP decoding library for Solidity (e.g., Hamdiallah/RLP or custom) — audit before use
Finalized IdentityRegistry storage layout locked before deployment (slot numbers must not shift)
pot16.ptau and existing circuit artifacts unaffected — this is contract-only scope
